statement.p: Solve the following system of equations by graphing.

solution.p: Both equations are written in slope-intercept form. The first equation, y=\frac{2}{3}x-5\text{,} has slope \frac{2}{3} and y-intercept (0,-5)\text{.} The second equation, y=-\frac{1}{2}x+2\text{,} has slope -\frac{1}{2} and y-intercept (0,2)\text{.} We can use this information to graph both lines.

image.shortdescription: a Cartesian grid with two intersecting lines; one line has a y-intercept of -5 and a slope of 2/3; the other line has a y-intercept of 2 and a slope of -1/2

solution.p: It appears that the two lines intersect where x=6 and y=-1\text{,} so the solution to the system of equations would be the point (6,-1)\text{.} However we should check that (6,-1) actually works as a solution to each of the original equations.
This verifies that (6,-1) is the solution.
